questions.question: What are the three concentric layers of the Earth?
questions.explanation: The Earth is made of several layers, with the crust being the outermost and the core being the innermost.

questions.question: Which is the thinnest layer of the Earth?
questions.explanation: It is only about 35 km thick on the continental masses and only 5 km on the ocean floors.

questions.question: What are the main mineral constituents of the continental mass?
questions.explanation: The name 'Sial' comes from 'Si' for silica and 'Al' for alumina.

questions.question: What is the oceanic crust mainly composed of?
questions.explanation: It is called 'Sima' using 'Si' for silica and 'Ma' for magnesium.

questions.question: What is the innermost layer of the Earth called and what is it made of?
questions.correct_answer: The Core; made of Nickel and Iron (Nife)
questions.explanation: The central core has a very high temperature and pressure; 'Nife' stands for Nickel (Ni) and Ferrous/Iron (Fe).

questions.question: How are Igneous rocks formed?
questions.correct_answer: By the cooling and solidification of molten magma
questions.explanation: They are also called primary rocks. Examples include Granite and Basalt.

questions.question: What is the difference between Extrusive and Intrusive igneous rocks?
questions.correct_answer: Extrusive cool on the surface; Intrusive cool deep inside the earth
questions.explanation: Extrusive rocks like basalt have a fine-grained structure, while intrusive rocks like granite have large grains.

questions.question: How are Sedimentary rocks formed?
questions.correct_answer: From sediments being compressed and hardened into layers
questions.explanation: Rocks break down into small fragments (sediments) which are transported by wind/water and deposited.

questions.question: What is the Rock Cycle?
questions.correct_answer: The process of transformation of rocks from one type to another
questions.explanation: Igneous rocks turn into sedimentary, which can turn into metamorphic under heat and pressure, which can then melt back into magma.

questions.question: What are fossils?
questions.correct_answer: The remains of dead plants and animals trapped in layers of rocks
questions.explanation: Fossils are primarily found in sedimentary rocks.

notes.title: Mantle Depth
notes.content: The mantle extends up to a depth of 2900 km below the crust.

notes.title: Metamorphic Change
notes.content: Under great heat and pressure, igneous and sedimentary rocks change into metamorphic rocks, like clay changing into slate.

notes.title: Core Radius
notes.content: The innermost layer, the core, has a radius of about 3500 km.

facts.fact: The deepest mine in the world is in South Africa, and it is about 4 km deep.
The crust forms only 1% of the volume of the earth, 15% consists of the mantle and 84% makes the core.
The Deccan plateau is made up of basalt rocks.
